reviewer_notes: Manual taint-trace of all 5 automated findings plus independent discovery of CSRF and Stored XSS vectors.

verdicts.reasoning: The taint scanner marks $settings = WC_Order_Export_Manage::use_ready_or_prepare_settings($_POST) as a source and echo json_encode(...) as a sink. However: (1) check_nonce() is called at line 11 before any processing — this calls wp_verify_nonce() with the 'woe_nonce' nonce, and the gate also requires current_user_can('view_woocommerce_reports'). (2) The echo json_encode() at line 31 outputs numeric data ($total = integer result of build_file) and $html (HTML preview content). (3) json_encode() escapes JSON-special characters, so there is no raw HTML injection via the JSON envelope itself. (4) The HTML preview content ($html) could carry unsanitized HTML from order data or from user-controlled settings fields like header_text/footer_text — but this is a separate and more specific vulnerability (see CONFIRMED finding woo-order-export-lite-stored-xss-NEW-001) that the original flow_id did not accurately describe. The original flow as stated (POST -> build_file -> json_encode sink) is not directly exploitable as described.

Same structural false positive as xss-001. The sink is echo json_encode(array('total' => $total)) where $total is the return value of WC_Order_Export_Engine::build_file() in 'estimate' mode — this returns an integer (count of matching orders). An integer wrapped in json_encode produces safe JSON. No user-controlled string is reflected. nonce is verified, auth required.

Sink is echo json_encode(array('total' => $result['total'], 'file_id' => $file_id, 'max_line_items' => $result['max_line_items'], 'max_coupons' => $result['max_coupons'])). The values are: $result['total'] (integer), $file_id = current_time('timestamp') (integer timestamp set by server), $result['max_line_items'] and $result['max_coupons'] (integers). None of these values carry user-controlled strings. Nonce verified, auth required.

Source is $start = isset($_POST['start']) ? intval(sanitize_text_field(wp_unslash($_POST['start']))) : 0. The value is explicitly cast to integer via intval(). The sink is echo json_encode(array('start' => $start + $main_settings['ajax_orders_per_step'])). Adding two integers and json_encoding produces a numeric JSON value — not exploitable for XSS under any browser model. Nonce also verified.

Source is $settings = WC_Order_Export_Manage::make_new_settings($_POST). Sink is echo json_encode($error ? array('error' => $error) : array('id' => $id)). The success path outputs $id which is an integer (return of save_export_settings). The error path outputs $error = $ex->getMessage() where $ex is a PHP Exception thrown internally by save_export_settings — this method does not incorporate user-controlled values into exception messages (it performs DB reads/writes and scheduling). Furthermore, the JS at buttons.js:402 does inject response.error via jQuery.html(), which would be concerning if $error carried user input — but it does not (save_export_settings cannot throw exceptions with attacker-controlled messages). Additionally, nonce is verified via check_nonce() before any processing. False positive.

additional_findings.title: Stored XSS via HTML Export header_text / footer_text in Preview
additional_findings.description: When generating an HTML-format export preview (ajax_preview action), the HTML formatter (class-woe-formatter-html.php) writes $this->settings['header_text'] and $this->settings['footer_text'] directly into HTML output without sanitization (lines 69, 86, 151, 176, 196). These settings values originate from $_POST['settings']['header_text'] etc., which flow through make_new_settings() without sanitization. The resulting HTML is captured via ob_get_contents() and returned as response.html in the JSON response. The JavaScript at buttons.js:20 inserts this value via jQuery('#output_preview').html(html) — a direct innerHTML injection. An attacker with sufficient privilege (view_woocommerce_reports + valid nonce) can inject arbitrary HTML/JS via header_text or footer_text in a crafted preview request, causing XSS in the admin's browser.
additional_findings.nonce_obtainable: Yes — the nonce 'woe_nonce' is embedded in settings_form.woe_nonce in page source for any authenticated user visiting the plugin admin page
additional_findings.stored_or_reflected: Reflected (in preview mode, settings are not saved). Can become stored XSS if a malicious export profile is saved with malicious header_text and another admin previews it.
additional_findings.exploitability: Medium — requires authentication and nonce, but nonce is accessible to any authenticated user who can visit WooCommerce admin. Escalation path: shop manager injects JS that captures nonces/cookies of viewing admins.

additional_findings.title: CSRF on ajax_export_download_bulk_file — Missing Nonce for No-Tab Ajax Route
additional_findings.description: In ajax_gate() (class-wc-order-export-admin.php:512), the routing logic has two branches: (a) if $tab is not set or not a valid tab key, the request is routed to WC_Order_Export_Ajax::$method() WITHOUT any nonce check; (b) if $tab is a valid tab, check_admin_referer('woe_nonce', 'woe_nonce') is called. The ajax_export_download_bulk_file method in WC_Order_Export_Ajax has no internal nonce check (contrast with ajax_run_one_job which calls check_ajax_referer). Only the capability check (view_woocommerce_reports or export_woocommerce_orders) remains. This means any page that can trick an authenticated WooCommerce manager/admin into visiting it can forge a cross-site request that triggers a full order export. The plugin is registered as wp_ajax_order_exporter (authenticated only), so unauthenticated attackers cannot exploit this.
additional_findings.impact: An attacker can trigger order data export for a logged-in WooCommerce admin/manager. If the export destination is configured as browser download, the file goes to the victim's browser (limited impact in CSRF). If export destination is FTP/email (Pro feature), an attacker could potentially redirect exports to attacker-controlled infrastructure by manipulating profile settings first.
additional_findings.exploitability: Low-Medium — practical impact is limited in the free plugin (browser download only). Elevated in Pro with remote export destinations.
